fresh_facts.claim: The US deployed approximately 2,500 Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit aboard the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli to the Middle East for crisis response and maritime security.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth approved the deployment of Marines to the Middle East at CENTCOM's request.
President Trump announced US strikes 'obliterated' military targets on Iran's Kharg Island and warned oil infrastructure could be next.
Israeli strikes on Iran are ongoing, described as 'pounding' Iranian targets, with continuing and intensifying strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon.
Iran is attacking Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure with missiles and drones, effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz and driving up oil prices.
Iran-aligned militias targeted the US Embassy helipad in Baghdad with missiles.
A drone strike by Iran-aligned militias in northern Iraq killed a French soldier.
Qatar intercepted most incoming Iranian missiles and drones.
Iranian security forces increased street presence to suppress dissent while encouraging pro-government demonstrations.
UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for the Middle East war to stop and urged full implementation of Security Council Resolution 2817 on March 18.
US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testified to the Senate on March 18 that Iran's government 'appears intact but largely degraded' due to attacks.
Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi stated on March 18 that Iran's nuclear doctrine remains unchanged despite losses.
Saudi Arabia hosted an emergency meeting of Arab and Islamic foreign ministers in Riyadh on March 18 after intercepting Iranian ballistic missiles.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died on February 28, 2026, and his son Mojtaba Khamenei was rapidly elevated.
Israel killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib in a Tehran strike reported March 18.
Ali Larijani, Iran's security chief, and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani were killed in a prior Israeli strike, with funerals held March 18-19.
Iranian leaders including Judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, President Masoud Pezeshkian, and Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf vowed revenge and continuity after leadership losses.
Foreign Minister Araqchi claimed Iran's government system remains 'structurally strong' despite individual losses on March 18.
The NCRI announced a six-month transitional government plan post-regime fall on March 18, limited to organizing elections.
Israeli strikes hit South Pars gas complex and Asaluyeh facilities on March 18, causing fires and halting gas to Iraq.
Brent crude oil surged above $108 per barrel following strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure on March 18.
Qatar and UAE condemned the escalation of strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure on March 18.
Iran's President Pezeshkian discussed de-escalation with Egypt's El-Sissi in a Friday phone call.
President Trump urged Iranian opponents to 'take over' the government post-bombardment but noted a 'big hurdle' to regime change.
Israel noted promoting anti-regime protests amid the ongoing war on March 18.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces cascading crises following Mojtaba Khamenei's succession: senior military and intelligence leadership has been systematically eliminated through coordinated US-Israeli strikes, critical energy infrastructure including South Pars gas complex has been damaged, the Strait of Hormuz is contested, and internal security forces are escalating suppression measures while attempting to counter growing anti-regime sentiment. Despite regime claims of structural integrity, US intelligence assessment confirms significant degradation, and the new leadership's continuation of confrontational posturing amid material weakness creates unprecedented instability.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli and US forces conducted comprehensive strikes on March 18-19 targeting Iranian military command structures, energy infrastructure (South Pars gas complex, Kharg Island, Asaluyeh facilities), and killing senior officials including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, Security Chief Ali Larijani, and Basij Chief Gholamreza Soleimani, while 2,500 Marines positioned for regional deployment and Brent crude surged above $108 per barrel.
